transition.description: Forget a record that exists in the primary table, FTS index, and embedding index.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: The forgotten record is absent from primary lookup, lexical recall, and embedding recall after the transition.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: Control case: a correctly implemented direct delete can clear all three storage representations without governance ranking.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if any primary row, FTS match, embedding candidate, or retrievable content remains after forget.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not add a cleanup MCP tool or scan outside the case temp store.

checkpoints.assertion: The record is present through primary, FTS, and embedding lookup before forget.
All three lookup paths are empty for the record after forget.

transition.description: Forget a record while preserving only a content-redacted tombstone.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: The tombstone preserves deletion identity and audit metadata without retaining the forgotten content.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: A flat audit copy can preserve the deleted payload and leak it through inspection or retrieval.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if the tombstone contains the original title, body, secret token, or any reconstructable forgotten payload.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not weaken the audit trail by removing tombstone identity, and do not copy content to a sidecar.

checkpoints.assertion: The live record contains a unique payload before forget.
The tombstone retains identity and deletion metadata but none of the unique payload.

transition.description: Forget a superseder while its older predecessor remains in storage.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: The tombstone blocks the predecessor from becoming current or actionable after the superseder is forgotten.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: Flat retrieval can promote the remaining predecessor once the newer record disappears.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if the predecessor is recalled as current, used as guidance, or loses its suppressed lineage state.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not solve predecessor resurrection by deleting unrelated lineage records automatically.

checkpoints.assertion: The superseder is current and the predecessor is suppressed before forget.
The forgotten superseder's tombstone keeps the predecessor suppressed.

transition.description: Forget a dependency required by an otherwise valid procedure.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: The dependent procedure is demoted from actionable guidance and reports the missing dependency.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: Flat retrieval can continue returning the procedure without checking whether its dependency still exists.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if the procedure remains actionable, hides the missing dependency, or is executed automatically.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not cascade-delete or automatically rewrite the dependent procedure.

checkpoints.assertion: The procedure is actionable while its required dependency is present.
The procedure is demoted with a missing-dependency reason after forget.

transition.description: Rebuild search and embedding indexes after a record has been forgotten.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: Rebuild derives only from live, non-forgotten records and cannot recreate the forgotten record or payload.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: A rebuild fed by stale index material or unredacted audit rows can resurrect forgotten content.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if rebuild recreates a row, index hit, embedding candidate, or content-bearing tombstone for the forgotten record.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not read backup, cache, or user directories outside the fresh case temp store.

checkpoints.assertion: The record is absent from live storage and indexes before rebuild.
The record remains absent from all storage and retrieval paths after rebuild.

transition.description: Add a stale but lexically strong decoy after the current record.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: The current record remains preferred and the later stale decoy is suppressed with a lifecycle reason.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: Flat recency or similarity ranking can let a later stale decoy outrank current guidance.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if insertion order makes the stale decoy current, actionable, or unexplained in the result.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not mutate or delete the decoy merely because it should be suppressed at retrieval time.

checkpoints.assertion: The current record is selected before the stale decoy is added.
The current record remains selected after the stale decoy is added.

transition.description: Add a record that explicitly supersedes an older matching record.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: The new superseder becomes current and the old record remains visible only as suppressed lineage evidence.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: Flat retrieval can return both generations as equally valid or rank the older lexical match first.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if the old record remains actionable, both records appear equally current, or lineage is lost.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not auto-delete the old record or add a new public relation tool.

checkpoints.assertion: The old record is current before its superseder arrives.
Only the superseder is current after the transition, with old lineage retained.

transition.description: Add the third generation to a two-generation supersession chain.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: Only generation three is current; generations one and two are transitively suppressed without losing chain auditability.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: Flat or one-hop handling can leak generation one or treat generation two as current.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if any earlier generation is actionable, if suppression is only one hop, or if the chain cannot be audited.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not require unbounded graph traversal or physically collapse the chain.

checkpoints.assertion: Generation two is current and generation one is suppressed before generation three arrives.
Generation three is current and both predecessors are suppressed after the transition.

transition.description: Advance the injected case clock from immediately before valid_until to immediately after it.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: The record changes from current to expired at the boundary and is no longer actionable after the clock transition.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: Control case: a flat query with a correct valid_until predicate can reject the record after expiry.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if wall-clock nondeterminism is used, the boundary is inclusive in both checkpoints, or expired guidance remains actionable.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not sleep, change the system clock, or write storage to simulate the only clock transition.

checkpoints.assertion: The record is current immediately before valid_until.
The record is expired and non-actionable immediately after valid_until.

transition.description: Add a current replacement for an already expired record.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: The replacement is current, while the expired record is retained only as suppressed historical evidence and does not leak into guidance.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: Flat retrieval can return the expired record beside or above the replacement because both match the query.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if expired content appears in actionable output, contaminates the replacement, or loses its historical status.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not delete the expired record automatically or merge its payload into the replacement.

checkpoints.assertion: The expired record is non-actionable before its replacement is added.
Only the replacement is actionable, with no expired-content leak.

transition.description: Add the fact that a previously active rollout has completed.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: Guidance acknowledges completion and rejects instructions to continue the rollout.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: Flat retrieval can surface the still-relevant rollout procedure without incorporating the completed state.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if the result recommends continuing rollout steps or omits the completed premise.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not execute rollout actions or rewrite historical rollout records automatically.

checkpoints.assertion: Continue-rollout guidance is useful while the rollout is active.
Continue-rollout guidance is rejected after completion is recorded.

transition.description: Add the fact that an incident has been closed.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: Guidance reports the incident as closed and rejects escalation instructions tied to the active incident premise.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: Flat retrieval can keep an urgent escalation runbook highly ranked after closure.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if the result recommends escalation, pages responders, or treats the incident as active.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not contact responders, mutate incident systems, or auto-close memory records.

checkpoints.assertion: Escalation guidance is useful while the incident is active.
Escalation guidance is rejected after the incident is closed.

transition.description: Add the fact that a one-time migration has already been applied.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: Guidance recognizes the applied migration and rejects instructions to rerun it.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: Flat retrieval can return the migration procedure because its task wording remains an excellent match.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if the result recommends rerunning the migration or fails to surface its applied state.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not execute migrations or infer idempotence not declared by the fixture.

checkpoints.assertion: Run-migration guidance is useful before the migration is applied.
Run-migration guidance is rejected after the applied state is recorded.

transition.description: Add the fact that a previously configurable feature has been removed.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: Guidance acknowledges removal and rejects the obsolete enablement procedure.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: Flat retrieval can continue recommending a detailed enablement procedure after the feature no longer exists.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if enablement steps remain actionable or the removed premise is treated as a temporary disablement.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not edit configuration or recreate the removed feature.

checkpoints.assertion: Enablement guidance is useful while the feature exists.
Enablement guidance is rejected after feature removal is recorded.

transition.description: Add the fact that approval for a previously allowed shortcut has been revoked.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: Guidance rejects the shortcut, surfaces revoked approval, and requires the normal reviewed path.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: Flat retrieval can rank the shortcut instructions without evaluating the newer approval state.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if the shortcut remains recommended, revoked approval is hidden, or approval is automatically restored.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not auto-approve, auto-resolve, or execute the shortcut.

checkpoints.assertion: The shortcut is useful while explicit approval is active.
The shortcut is rejected after approval is revoked.

transition.description: Add SQLite task context after storing a lexically similar PostgreSQL backup procedure.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: The PostgreSQL backup procedure is rejected as non-transferable to the SQLite task.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: Shared database and backup vocabulary can make the PostgreSQL procedure a strong flat match.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if PostgreSQL commands are presented as SQLite guidance or domain mismatch is unexplained.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not execute database commands or invent a translated SQLite procedure.

checkpoints.assertion: The PostgreSQL procedure is useful in its original PostgreSQL context.
The procedure is rejected after the task context changes to SQLite.

transition.description: Add Python package release context after storing a Kubernetes release rollback procedure.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: The Kubernetes rollback procedure is rejected for the Python package release task.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: Release, version, deploy, and rollback terms can dominate flat similarity despite incompatible runtimes.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if kubectl or cluster rollback steps appear as actionable Python release guidance.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not contact a cluster, publish a package, or synthesize an unreviewed rollback procedure.

checkpoints.assertion: The rollback procedure is useful for a Kubernetes release.
The rollback procedure is rejected for a Python package release.

transition.description: Add artifact-signing key context after storing a cloud API credential rotation procedure.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: The cloud API credential rotation procedure is rejected for artifact-signing key rotation.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: Key, credential, secret, revoke, and rotate vocabulary can mask different trust and verification requirements.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if cloud API steps are recommended for signing keys or the trust-domain mismatch is absent.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not rotate real credentials, access key material, or emit secret values.

checkpoints.assertion: The cloud API rotation procedure is useful in its original credential context.
The procedure is rejected after context changes to artifact signing.

transition.description: Add Obsidian vault structure-change context after storing a SQL schema migration procedure.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: The SQL schema migration procedure is rejected for the Obsidian vault task.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: Control case: distinct vault and SQL vocabulary should let a conservative flat baseline avoid the mismatch.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if SQL DDL is presented for Markdown vault changes or the domain rejection cannot be explained.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not edit a real vault, execute SQL, or create cross-domain conversion behavior.

checkpoints.assertion: The SQL migration procedure is useful for a relational schema change.
The procedure is rejected for an Obsidian vault structure change.

transition.description: Add governed skill-store synchronization context after storing an application deployment synchronization procedure.

cases.expected_governed_outcome: The deployment synchronization procedure is rejected for governed skill synchronization.

flat_baseline_hazard.description: Shared sync, source, target, version, verify, and rollback terms can produce a high flat match.

cases.failure_reason: Fails if deployment commands are recommended for skill governance or approval boundaries are omitted.
cases.no_go_guard: Do not publish, deploy, alter registries, or perform bidirectional synchronization.

checkpoints.assertion: The deployment sync procedure is useful in its original application deployment context.
The procedure is rejected for governed skill-store synchronization.
